name: Sierra de Alor Circular Trail from Olivenza
name_original: Mirador Sierra de Alor — circular desde Olivenza
description: Explore the Mirador Sierra de Alor on this 15.0-mile moderate touring cycling route from Olivenza, featuring panoramic views.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Olivenza traverse the dehesa and extensive oak and cork oak forests, characteristic of Spain's Badajoz province. The region features small mountain ranges and hills, such as the Sierra de Alor, offering varied terrain. Cyclists can also find routes along the Guadiana River and near the Great Lake of Alqueva, Western Europe's largest artificial lake. This landscape provides a mix of natural backdrops for cycling. Best touring cycling routes around Olivenza The most popular touring cycling route is Mirador Sierra de Alor — circular from Olivenza, a 15.0 miles (24.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 7 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Sierra de Alor, a Special Conservation Area. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Albalá – Bridge Over the Olivenza River loop from Los Cuarteles, a moderate 31.1 miles (50.0 km) path. This route follows the Olivenza River, providing a scenic ride through the local landscape. Local touring cyclists also love the View of the Alqueva Reservoir loop from Vale Sagrado do Lucefecit, a 18.1 miles (29.2 km) trail leading through the area surrounding the Great Lake of Alqueva, often completed in about 1 hour 57 minutes. Touring cycling around Olivenza is defined by dehesa landscapes, oak forests, and the Guadiana River basin. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes with elevation gains. The routes in Olivenza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Olivenza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Olivenza?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes available around Olivenza, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix, with 6 easy routes, 13 moderate routes, and 4 more challenging options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Olivenza?
A: The terrain around Olivenza is quite varied. You'll encounter the characteristic 'dehesa' landscape with extensive oak and cork oak forests, small mountain ranges like the Sierras de Alor and Monte Longo, and routes along the Guadiana River and near the Great Lake of Alqueva. Routes feature a mix of asphalt roads, forested trails, and paths, offering diverse experiences for touring cyclists.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Olivenza?
A: Yes, Olivenza offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners. For instance, the area has 6 routes classified as easy, providing a gentle introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the Olivenza area?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022416911/mirador-sierra-de-alor-circular-from-olivenza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Sierra de Alor — circular from Olivenza&lt;/a&gt;, which offers views of the Sierra de Alor.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints I can see while cycling?
A: While cycling around Olivenza, you can enjoy the unique dehesa landscape, extensive oak forests, and the Guadiana River basin. A significant natural feature is the Great Lake of Alqueva, Western Europe's largest artificial lake. You can experience this on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31495522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Alqueva Reservoir loop from Vale Sagrado do Lucefecit&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3128829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Alor Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; also offers panoramic views of the Special Conservation Area.

Q: Are there historical sites or cultural attractions along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, Olivenza is rich in history and culture due to its Spanish and Portuguese influences. You can explore sites like the Medieval Citadel and Alcázar, which houses the González Santana Ethnographic Museum, or admire the Manueline architecture of the Church of Santa María Magdalena. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1577341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Confluence of the Caya and Guadiana Rivers&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable spot. Many routes pass by or near these fascinating landmarks.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Olivenza?
A: The best times for touring cycling in Olivenza are winter, spring, and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is generally more pleasant for outdoor activities. In April, you might even catch the beautiful flowering of the rose of Alexandria.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Olivenza?
A: The touring cycling routes around Olivenza are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.25 stars from more than 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic dehesa landscapes, and the well-maintained paths that offer a mix of natural beauty and cultural exploration.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 4 difficult touring cycling routes around Olivenza. One such route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38153647" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Jorge de Alor – Sierra de Alor Viewpoint loop from Valverde de Leganés&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation gains.

Q: Can I find routes that follow the Olivenza River?
A: Yes, you can find scenic routes that follow the Olivenza River. A great option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38153883" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albalá – Bridge Over the Olivenza River loop from Los Cuarteles&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 50 km path that provides a beautiful ride through the local landscape alongside the river.

Q: Are there any castles or fortifications to explore near the cycling routes?
A: The region around Olivenza, being a historic frontier zone, features numerous defensive buildings and castles. You can explore the Medieval Citadel in Olivenza itself, or visit nearby structures like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5529327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Miraflores&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7894603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castelo de Juromenha&lt;/a&gt;. These historical sites add a rich cultural dimension to your cycling adventure.
tips: Just six kilometers from Olivenza, this small town is surrounded by fields and hills. The streets are narrow and the buildings are whitewashed. The center is interesting for the monumental chimneys of many of the buildings.
Located 6 km from Olivenza, is the Sierra de Alor, declared a Special Conservation Area, due to its great ecological value and its good state of conservation.

Olivenza is a historical and cultural gem in the province of Badajoz, with a unique blend of Spanish and Portuguese influences.

Olivenza was Portuguese for over 500 years (1297-1801) and still preserves bilingual inscriptions and Portuguese architecture.
Portugal continues to symbolically claim sovereignty, even though the town is fully integrated into Spain.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to cycle the Mirador Sierra de Alor circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 24 km long and generally takes around 2 hours and 7 minutes to complete by touring bicycle, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as moderate. While it starts with relatively easy, flat sections through olive groves, it becomes more challenging with climbing paths as you approach the Sierra de Alor. Some sections may have loose material, so a basic level of cycling fitness and experience is recommended.

faqs.question: Where does the cycling route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route from Olivenza. You can typically start from various points within or near Olivenza. Parking is generally available in the town itself, which serves as a convenient base for the route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. You'll start on relatively flat sections through olive groves. As you climb towards the Sierra de Alor, the paths become more challenging, with some sections offering a good, firm surface. However, be aware that some descents, if you choose more adventurous options, can involve narrow trails with loose material and dry torrent beds.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse and beautiful landscapes. The initial parts are flanked by olive groves, which give way to cork oaks as you gain elevation, providing welcome shade. A major highlight is the Sierra de Alor Viewpoint, offering spectacular panoramic views of the Los Llanos de Olivenza region. The area is also known for its rich flora, including the unique wild peony in spring.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to cycle this route are during spring and autumn. Spring is particularly recommended to witness the vibrant flora, including the 'Rosa de Alejandría' (wild peony) which blooms in April. Autumn offers pleasant temperatures and beautiful colors.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Mirador Sierra de Alor cycling route?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the panoramic Sierra de Alor Viewpoint, traditional 'chozos' (shepherd's huts), and the possibility of encountering the historical refuge of the bandit Diego Corrientes. You can also make a short detour to the ancient Fuente de Val do Gral. The route also passes through the settlement of San Jorge de Alor.

faqs.question: Does this cycling route intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other local paths. You will cycle along parts of the Plaza de Luis González Chacón, Camino de Olivenza a Táliga, and Plaza de Hispanoamérica.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Mirador Sierra de Alor cycling route?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions mentioned, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near settlements like San Jorge de Alor, to ensure their safety and respect for the natural environment.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle in the Sierra de Alor?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Alor is a declared Special Conservation Area, but there are typically no permits or entrance fees required for cycling on established routes. However, always respect local regulations and conservation efforts.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a touring bicycle trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's advisable to pack sufficient water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit, and appropriate cycling gear. Sun protection is important, and layers are recommended for changing temperatures, especially in spring and autumn. Don't forget your camera for the views from the Sierra de Alor Viewpoint!

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the Mirador Sierra de Alor cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Olivenza, where you'll find various cafes and restaurants. The route also passes through San Jorge de Alor, which may offer limited options. It's best to carry enough provisions for the ride itself.
